mnemonics: Deep Divers Sink First (DFS) - They go as deep as possible before coming back up to try a new branch.
keyPoints: DFS uses a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) stack data structure.
It explores a path as far as possible before backtracking.
It is not optimal and not complete for uninformed search in infinite spaces.
It has a low memory requirement, storing only the current path.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that DFS can get stuck in infinite loops without a cycle-checking mechanism.
Confusing the LIFO stack (DFS) with the FIFO queue used in Breadth-First Search (BFS).
Assuming DFS will always find the shortest path; it often does not.
